title: Fix missing NULL guards in C extension call and descriptor slots
author: GrahamDumpleton
body:
## Summary

The C extension's `WraptFunctionWrapperBase_call`, `WraptFunctionWrapperBase_descr_get`,
and `WraptBoundFunctionWrapper_call` functions were missing the standard
`if (!self->object_proxy.wrapped)` NULL guard that other slots use. If a wrapper object
was constructed via `__new__` without calling `__init__`, calling or accessing the
descriptor on the uninitialized object would dereference NULL pointers, resulting in a
crash (SIGSEGV) rather than raising `WrapperNotInitializedError`. Added the same guard
pattern already used throughout the rest of the C extension to these three functions.

## Credits

Thanks to @devdanzin for the report via:

* https://gist.github.com/devdanzin/34c9fad09694958b83fbbea19ea30e69
